texto: I don't think I could compare myself to Macaulay Culkin, because we're pretty much two different kinds of actors. He's done a lot of comedy. He does mostly just comedy like 'Uncle Buck' and 'Home Alone' and 'Home Alone 2.' And I've done a lot of different stuff, like sad movies, like the movie about the kid with AIDS.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Brian Bonsall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't think I could compare myself to Macaulay Culkin, because we're pretty much two different …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, home, myself pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de myself e sad.

frase_original: I don't think I could compare myself to Macaulay Culkin, because we're pretty much two different kinds of actors. He's done a lot of comedy. He does mostly just comedy like 'Uncle Buck' and 'Home Alone' and 'Home Alone 2.' And I've done a lot of different stuff, like sad movies, like the movie about the kid with AIDS.
